title: triticum rottboellioides
intro: Triticum rottboellioides, also known as Wild Einkorn or Rottboell's Wild Einkorn, is a type of wild wheat that belongs to the grass family and is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia, Africa, and Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Triticum rottboellioides include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and soothe skin irritations. It can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, and is also used in traditional remedies to treat various health conditions.
